title: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our classic recipe. Creamy, rich, and flavorful, this Indian favorite is easier than you think! Get the step-by-step guide.
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of classic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with rice and naan bread, garnished with fresh cilantro.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, creamy flavors of our classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani), a beloved Indian dish perfect for any occasion.

description: Experience the beloved taste of Butter Chicken, a rich and creamy Indian curry featuring tender chicken in a subtly spiced tomato-based sauce. This Murgh Makhani recipe delivers an authentic restaurant-quality dish right in your home. Perfect for a comforting meal, it pairs wonderfully with naan or basmati rice.

ingredients.item: Boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 minutes

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine chicken pieces with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, Kashmiri red chili powder, garam masala, and salt. Mix well to coat all the chicken. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 2 tablespoons of butter in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces and cook in batches until browned and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes per batch. Set aside the cooked chicken.

instructions.title: Prepare the Gravy Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, add the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter. Once melted, add the finely chopped onion and sauté until translucent and lightly golden, about 5-7 minutes. Add the remaining 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Simmer the Tomato Sauce
instructions.text: Stir in the tomato puree, 1 tsp Kashmiri red chili powder, 1 tsp garam masala, and salt to taste. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 10-15 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the sauce to thicken slightly. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Blend and Finish the Sauce
instructions.text: Drain the soaked cashews and blend them with a little water to form a smooth paste. Add the cashew paste to the tomato sauce. If desired, you can blend the entire sauce for an extra smooth texture before adding cashews. Stir in the fresh cream, sugar, and crushed fenugreek leaves (Kasuri Methi).

instructions.title: Combine and Serve
instructions.text: Add the cooked chicken pieces back into the creamy sauce. Simmer for another 5-7 minutes, ensuring the chicken is heated through and coated well. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed. Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro before serving.

tips: For an extra smooth gravy, blend the cooked tomato-onion base with the soaked cashews before adding cream and chicken.
Always toast the Kasuri Methi (dried fenugreek leaves) lightly in a dry pan for a minute before crushing and adding to the curry to enhance its aroma.
If you prefer a spicier Butter Chicken, add a finely chopped green chili along with the onions in step 3, or increase the Kashmiri red chili powder slightly.
article: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani is a beloved Indian Chicken Recipes dish enjoyed across Indian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
